# gofakeit

```go
import "github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/v7"
```

Package gofakeit provides a set of functions that generate random data

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
Seed(11)

fmt.Println("Name:", Name())
fmt.Println("Email:", Email())
fmt.Println("Phone:", Phone())
fmt.Println("Address:", Address().Address)
fmt.Println("BS:", BS())
fmt.Println("Beer Name:", BeerName())
fmt.Println("Color:", Color())
fmt.Println("Company:", Company())
fmt.Println("Credit Card:", CreditCardNumber(nil))
fmt.Println("Hacker Phrase:", HackerPhrase())
fmt.Println("Job Title:", JobTitle())
fmt.Println("Password:", Password(true, true, true, true, false, 32))

// Output: Name: Priscilla Thornton
// Email: brookeduncan@maxwell.biz
// Phone: 7598907999
// Address: 4737 Port Hillstown, Santa Ana, Alabama 41026
// BS: interactive
// Beer Name: Hercules Double IPA
// Color: Chartreuse
// Company: Municode
// Credit Card: 6449062052570938760
// Hacker Phrase: I'll override the back-end USB system, that should encrypt the SCSI port!
// Job Title: Journalist
// Password: @M85m-v-4Nt*4T4.qsuw!.MC1Y68i@D.
```

#### Output

```
Name: Priscilla Thornton
Email: brookeduncan@maxwell.biz
Phone: 7598907999
Address: 4737 Port Hillstown, Santa Ana, Alabama 41026
BS: interactive
Beer Name: Hercules Double IPA
Color: Chartreuse
Company: Municode
Credit Card: 6449062052570938760
Hacker Phrase: I'll override the back-end USB system, that should encrypt the SCSI port!
Job Title: Journalist
Password: @M85m-v-4Nt*4T4.qsuw!.MC1Y68i@D.
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Custom)</summary>
<p>



```go
Seed(11)

AddFuncLookup("friendname", Info{
	Category:    "custom",
	Description: "Random friend name",
	Example:     "bill",
	Output:      "string",
	Generate: func(f *Faker, m *MapParams, info *Info) (any, error) {
		return RandomString([]string{"bill", "bob", "sally"}), nil
	},
})

type Foo struct {
	FriendName string `fake:"{friendname}"`
}

var f Foo
Struct(&f)

fmt.Printf("%s", f.FriendName)

// Output: sally
```

#### Output

```
sally
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Custom_with_params)</summary>
<p>



```go
Seed(11)

AddFuncLookup("jumbleword", Info{
	Category:    "jumbleword",
	Description: "Take a word and jumple it up",
	Example:     "loredlowlh",
	Output:      "string",
	Params: []Param{
		{Field: "word", Type: "int", Description: "Word you want to jumble"},
	},
	Generate: func(f *Faker, m *MapParams, info *Info) (any, error) {
		word, err := info.GetString(m, "word")
		if err != nil {
			return nil, err
		}

		split := strings.Split(word, "")
		f.ShuffleStrings(split)
		return strings.Join(split, ""), nil
	},
})

type Foo struct {
	JumbleWord string `fake:"{jumbleword:helloworld}"`
}

var f Foo
Struct(&f)

fmt.Printf("%s", f.JumbleWord)

// Output: hldoolewrl
```

#### Output

```
hldoolewrl
```

</p>
</details>

## Variables

<a name="FuncLookups"></a>FuncLookups is the primary map array with mapping to all available data

```go
var FuncLookups map[string]Info
```

<a name="RecursiveDepth"></a>RecursiveDepth controls the maximum recursion depth when populating structs. Increase if your data structures are deeply nested; decrease to be more conservative.

```go
var RecursiveDepth = 10
```

<a name="APIUserAgent"></a>
## func [APIUserAgent](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/internet.go#L248>)

```go
func APIUserAgent() string
```

APIUserAgent will generate a random API user agent string

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
Seed(11)
fmt.Println(APIUserAgent())

// Output: httpie-go/8.1.2
```

#### Output

```
httpie-go/8.1.2
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="CSV"></a>
## func [CSV](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/csv.go#L22>)

```go
func CSV(co *CSVOptions) ([]byte, error)
```

CSV generates an object or an array of objects in json format A nil CSVOptions returns a randomly structured CSV.

<details><summary>Example (Array)</summary>
<p>



```go
Seed(11)

value, err := CSV(&CSVOptions{
	RowCount: 3,
	Fields: []Field{
		{Name: "id", Function: "autoincrement"},
		{Name: "first_name", Function: "firstname"},
		{Name: "last_name", Function: "lastname"},
		{Name: "password", Function: "password", Params: MapParams{"special": {"false"}}},
	},
})
if err != nil {
	fmt.Println(err)
}

fmt.Println(string(value))

// Output: id,first_name,last_name,password
// 1,Priscilla,Thornton,F395YfK9tHs9
// 2,Emmett,Black,PL0G37o1pY0p
// 3,Liza,Vincent,q11fR4MwsM4N
```

#### Output

```
id,first_name,last_name,password
1,Priscilla,Thornton,F395YfK9tHs9
2,Emmett,Black,PL0G37o1pY0p
3,Liza,Vincent,q11fR4MwsM4N
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="CreditCardNumber"></a>
## func [CreditCardNumber](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/payment.go#L102>)

```go

```

CreditCardNumber will generate a random luhn credit card number

<p>



```go

fmt.Println(CreditCardNumber(&CreditCardOptions{Gaps: true}))

// Output: 6376121252759896
// 6449344737930519
// 4111026894059156
// 6706 2052 5709 6
```

```
6376121252759896
6449344737930519
4111026894059156
6706 2052 5709 6
```

<a name="EmailText"></a>
## func [EmailText](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/template.go#L143>)

```go
func EmailText(co *EmailOptions) (string, error)
```

EmailText will return a single random text email template document

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
Seed(11)

value, err := EmailText(&EmailOptions{})
if err != nil {
	fmt.Println(err)
}

fmt.Println(string(value))

// Output: Subject: Hi from Nannie!
//
// Dear Duncan,
//
// Greetings! I hope your day is going well.
//
// I trust this email finds you well. Sending good vibes your way.
//
// Warm starts beat cold company. Launch the person midweek for clarity. Automate problem recovery victoriously. Launch the life midweek for clarity.
//
// Sometimes the point last never. Retire outdated life each quarter. Choose lovely defaults. Deliberately wake the part. Clear time were better than clever week.
//
// Warm starts beat cold child. Systematically improve the woman yearly. Protect the place under grumpy load.
//
// I'm curious to know what you think about it. If you have a moment, please feel free to check out the project on GitLab
//
// Feel free to share your opinions with me. Looking forward to your feedback!
//
// I appreciate your attention to this matter. Wishing you a wonderful day!
//
// Warm regards
// Jamal Elliott
// janierussell@bailey.io
// 1-875-720-1747
```

#### Output

```
Subject: Hi from Nannie!

Dear Duncan,

Greetings! I hope your day is going well.

I trust this email finds you well. Sending good vibes your way.

Warm starts beat cold company. Launch the person midweek for clarity. Automate problem recovery victoriously. Launch the life midweek for clarity.

Sometimes the point last never. Retire outdated life each quarter. Choose lovely defaults. Deliberately wake the part. Clear time were better than clever week.

Warm starts beat cold child. Systematically improve the woman yearly. Protect the place under grumpy load.

I'm curious to know what you think about it. If you have a moment, please feel free to check out the project on GitLab

Feel free to share your opinions with me. Looking forward to your feedback!

I appreciate your attention to this matter. Wishing you a wonderful day!

Warm regards
Jamal Elliott
janierussell@bailey.io
1-875-720-1747
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="FixedWidth"></a>
## func [FixedWidth](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/generate.go#L149>)

```go
func FixedWidth(co *FixedWidthOptions) (string, error)
```

FixedWidth generates an table of random data in fixed width format A nil FixedWidthOptions returns a randomly structured FixedWidth.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
Seed(11)

value, err := FixedWidth(&FixedWidthOptions{
	RowCount: 3,
	Fields: []Field{
		{Name: "Name", Function: "{firstname} {lastname}"},
		{Name: "Email", Function: "email"},
		{Name: "Password", Function: "password", Params: MapParams{"special": {"false"}}},
		{Name: "Age", Function: "{number:1,100}"},
	},
})
if err != nil {
	fmt.Println(err)
}

fmt.Println(string(value))

// Output: Name                   Email                          Password         Age
// Priscilla Thornton     laurelmcclure@wiley.org        93Ya9ABSa4kq     92
// Brooke Duncan          ramonblack@rutherford.io       US8ptM3c2T07     64
// Gregg Ford             roxannewalsh@hamilton.name     4wP4V90iMs44     59
```

#### Output

```
Name                   Email                          Password         Age
Priscilla Thornton     laurelmcclure@wiley.org        93Ya9ABSa4kq     92
Brooke Duncan          ramonblack@rutherford.io       US8ptM3c2T07     64
Gregg Ford             roxannewalsh@hamilton.name     4wP4V90iMs44     59
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Default)</summary>
<p>



```go
Seed(11)

value, err := FixedWidth(nil)
if err != nil {
	fmt.Println(err)
}

fmt.Println(string(value))

// Output: Name                  Email                        Password
// Nannie Duncan         lancegraves@sandoval.io      n7iSUr2MWt8c
// Brooklyn Maxwell      danykaadams@morgan.com       4v940LNiV44s
// Carmen Romero         robinjenkins@cormier.com     472CR3CDG0ke
// Hailie Wiley          buddypearson@summers.io      Hd692w44DlzL
// Marion Wallace        emmettblack@mcdermott.io     K187He5tUfFj
// Amalia Rutherford     aprilmuller@joseph.net       2ibBGsrc0mW3
// Roxane Webb           jaywashington@barton.net     s0HNN9fkEL1u
// Randy Hamilton        caseybartell@myers.biz       ORe9uGIuP4m0
// Elliott Jacobson      janierussell@bailey.io       Hd5Kl6T2g7pi
```

#### Output

```
Name                  Email                        Password
Nannie Duncan         lancegraves@sandoval.io      n7iSUr2MWt8c
Brooklyn Maxwell      danykaadams@morgan.com       4v940LNiV44s
Carmen Romero         robinjenkins@cormier.com     472CR3CDG0ke
Hailie Wiley          buddypearson@summers.io      Hd692w44DlzL
Marion Wallace        emmettblack@mcdermott.io     K187He5tUfFj
Amalia Rutherford     aprilmuller@joseph.net       2ibBGsrc0mW3
Roxane Webb           jaywashington@barton.net     s0HNN9fkEL1u
Randy Hamilton        caseybartell@myers.biz       ORe9uGIuP4m0
Elliott Jacobson      janierussell@bailey.io       Hd5Kl6T2g7pi
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (No Header)</summary>
<p>



```go
Seed(11)

value, err := FixedWidth(&FixedWidthOptions{
	RowCount: 3,
	Fields: []Field{
		{Name: "", Function: "{firstname} {lastname}"},
		{Name: "", Function: "email"},
		{Name: "", Function: "password", Params: MapParams{"special": {"false"}}},
		{Name: "", Function: "{number:1,100}"},
	},
})
if err != nil {
	fmt.Println(err)
}

fmt.Println(value)

// Output: Priscilla Thornton     laurelmcclure@wiley.org        93Ya9ABSa4kq     92
// Brooke Duncan          ramonblack@rutherford.io       US8ptM3c2T07     64
// Gregg Ford             roxannewalsh@hamilton.name     4wP4V90iMs44     59
```

#### Output

```
Priscilla Thornton     laurelmcclure@wiley.org        93Ya9ABSa4kq     92
Brooke Duncan          ramonblack@rutherford.io       US8ptM3c2T07     64
Gregg Ford             roxannewalsh@hamilton.name     4wP4V90iMs44     59
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Generate"></a>
## func [Generate](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/generate.go#L26>)

```go
func Generate(dataVal string) (string, error)
```

Generate fake information from given string. Replaceable values should be within \{\}

Functions Ex: \{firstname\} \- billy Ex: \{sentence:3\} \- Record river mind. Ex: \{number:1,10\} \- 4 Ex: \{uuid\} \- 590c1440\-9888\-45b0\-bd51\-a817ee07c3f2

Letters/Numbers Ex: \#\#\# \- 481 \- random numbers Ex: ??? \- fda \- random letters

For a complete list of runnable functions use FuncsLookup

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
Seed(11)

genStr, _ := Generate("{firstname} {lastname} {email} #?#?#?")
fmt.Println(genStr)
genStr, _ = Generate("{sentence}")
fmt.Println(genStr)
genStr, _ = Generate("{shuffleints:[1,2,3]}")
fmt.Println(genStr)
genStr, _ = Generate("{randomint:[1,2,3,-4]}")
fmt.Println(genStr)
genStr, _ = Generate("{randomuint:[1,2,3,4]}")
fmt.Println(genStr)
genStr, _ = Generate("{number:1,50}")
fmt.Println(genStr)
genStr, _ = Generate("{shufflestrings:[key:value,int:string,1:2,a:b]}")
fmt.Println(genStr)

// Output: Laurel McClure sethsoto@wallace.com 8K8b1M
// Automate problem recovery victoriously.
// [2 3 1]
// 2
// 4
// 16
// [int:string key:value a:b 1:2]
```

#### Output

```
Laurel McClure sethsoto@wallace.com 8K8b1M
Automate problem recovery victoriously.
[2 3 1]
2
4
16
[int:string key:value a:b 1:2]
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="JSON"></a>
## func [JSON](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/json.go#L61>)

```go
func JSON(jo *JSONOptions) ([]byte, error)
```

JSON generates an object or an array of objects in json format. A nil JSONOptions returns a randomly structured JSON.

<details><summary>Example (Array)</summary>
<p>



```go
Seed(11)

value, err := JSON(&JSONOptions{
	Type: "array",
	Fields: []Field{
		{Name: "id", Function: "autoincrement"},
		{Name: "first_name", Function: "firstname"},
		{Name: "last_name", Function: "lastname"},
		{Name: "password", Function: "password", Params: MapParams{"special": {"false"}}},
	},
	RowCount: 3,
	Indent:   true,
})
if err != nil {
	fmt.Println(err)
}

fmt.Println(string(value))

// Output: [
//     {
//         "id": 1,
//         "first_name": "Priscilla",
//         "last_name": "Thornton",
//         "password": "F395YfK9tHs9"
//     },
//     {
//         "id": 2,
//         "first_name": "Emmett",
//         "last_name": "Black",
//         "password": "PL0G37o1pY0p"
//     },
//     {
//         "id": 3,
//         "first_name": "Liza",
//         "last_name": "Vincent",
//         "password": "q11fR4MwsM4N"
//     }
// ]
```

#### Output

```
[
    {
        "id": 1,
        "first_name": "Priscilla",
        "last_name": "Thornton",
        "password": "F395YfK9tHs9"
    },
    {
        "id": 2,
        "first_name": "Emmett",
        "last_name": "Black",
        "password": "PL0G37o1pY0p"
    },
    {
        "id": 3,
        "first_name": "Liza",
        "last_name": "Vincent",
        "password": "q11fR4MwsM4N"
    }
]
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Number With Tag)</summary>
<p>



```go
Seed(11)

type J struct {
	FieldNumber  json.Number `fake:"number:3,7"`
	FieldInt8    json.Number `fake:"int8"`
	FieldInt16   json.Number `fake:"int16"`
	FieldInt32   json.Number `fake:"int32"`
	FieldInt64   json.Number `fake:"int64"`
	FieldUint8   json.Number `fake:"uint8"`
	FieldUint16  json.Number `fake:"uint16"`
	FieldUint32  json.Number `fake:"uint32"`
	FieldUint64  json.Number `fake:"uint64"`
	FieldFloat32 json.Number `fake:"float32"`
	FieldFloat64 json.Number `fake:"float64range:12,72"`
}

var obj J
Struct(&obj)

fmt.Printf("obj.FieldNumber = %+v\n", obj.FieldNumber)
fmt.Printf("obj.FieldInt8 = %+v\n", obj.FieldInt8)
fmt.Printf("obj.FieldInt16 = %+v\n", obj.FieldInt16)
fmt.Printf("obj.FieldInt32 = %+v\n", obj.FieldInt32)
fmt.Printf("obj.FieldInt64 = %+v\n", obj.FieldInt64)
fmt.Printf("obj.FieldUint8 = %+v\n", obj.FieldUint8)
fmt.Printf("obj.FieldUint16 = %+v\n", obj.FieldUint16)
fmt.Printf("obj.FieldUint32 = %+v\n", obj.FieldUint32)
fmt.Printf("obj.FieldUint64 = %+v\n", obj.FieldUint64)
fmt.Printf("obj.FieldFloat32 = %+v\n", obj.FieldFloat32)
fmt.Printf("obj.FieldFloat64 = %+v\n", obj.FieldFloat64)

// Output: obj.FieldNumber = 7
// obj.FieldInt8 = -110
// obj.FieldInt16 = 10933
// obj.FieldInt32 = 430103905
// obj.FieldInt64 = 525217394518216243
// obj.FieldUint8 = 164
// obj.FieldUint16 = 63417
// obj.FieldUint32 = 2307233133
// obj.FieldUint64 = 17560678512042153749
// obj.FieldFloat32 = 0.11857688426971436
// obj.FieldFloat64 = 51.03971481390635
```

#### Output

```
obj.FieldNumber = 7
obj.FieldInt8 = -110
obj.FieldInt16 = 10933
obj.FieldInt32 = 430103905
obj.FieldInt64 = 525217394518216243
obj.FieldUint8 = 164
obj.FieldUint16 = 63417
obj.FieldUint32 = 2307233133
obj.FieldUint64 = 17560678512042153749
obj.FieldFloat32 = 0.11857688426971436
obj.FieldFloat64 = 51.03971481390635
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Object)</summary>
<p>



```go
Seed(11)

value, err := JSON(&JSONOptions{
	Type: "object",
	Fields: []Field{
		{Name: "first_name", Function: "firstname"},
		{Name: "last_name", Function: "lastname"},
		{Name: "address", Function: "address"},
		{Name: "password", Function: "password", Params: MapParams{"special": {"false"}}},
	},
	Indent: true,
})
if err != nil {
	fmt.Println(err)
}

fmt.Println(string(value))

// Output: {
//     "first_name": "Priscilla",
//     "last_name": "Thornton",
//     "address": {
//         "address": "52759 Stationside, San Diego, Oregon 99344",
//         "street": "52759 Stationside",
//         "unit": "",
//         "city": "San Diego",
//         "state": "Oregon",
//         "zip": "99344",
//         "country": "France",
//         "latitude": 77.259885,
//         "longitude": 101.656736
//     },
//     "password": "1VAfP4oi5DLy"
// }
```

#### Output

```
{
    "first_name": "Priscilla",
    "last_name": "Thornton",
    "address": {
        "address": "52759 Stationside, San Diego, Oregon 99344",
        "street": "52759 Stationside",
        "unit": "",
        "city": "San Diego",
        "state": "Oregon",
        "zip": "99344",
        "country": "France",
        "latitude": 77.259885,
        "longitude": 101.656736
    },
    "password": "1VAfP4oi5DLy"
}
```

</p>
</details>


<p>



```go
Seed(11)
fmt.Println(LoremIpsumParagraph(3, 5, 12, "\n"))

// Output: Eveniet vero velit velit non incidunt est sed tenetur consequatur ut architecto. Quod non non et voluptas et esse quisquam ullam placeat molestiae laboriosam. <a name="Markdown"></a>
```
## func [Markdown](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/template.go#L93>)

```go
func Markdown(co *MarkdownOptions) (string, error)
```

Markdown will return a single random Markdown template document

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

TemplateMarkdown examples and tests

```go
Seed(11)

value, err := Markdown(&MarkdownOptions{})
if err != nil {
	fmt.Println(err)
}

fmt.Println(string(value))

// Output: # TurkeyThinker
//
// *Author: Roxane Webb*
//
// He open safely to stabilize the world. Automate problem recovery gracefully. Choose lovely defaults. Deliberately wake the part. Clear time were better than clever week.
//
// Warm starts beat cold child. Systematically improve the woman yearly. Protect the place under grumpy load.
//
// ## Table of Contents
// - [Installation](#installation)
// - [Usage](#usage)
// - [License](#license)
//
// ## Installation
// '''bash
// pip install TurkeyThinker
// '''
//
// ## Usage
// '''python
// result = turkeythinker.handle("whimsical story")
// print("turkeythinker result:", "terminated")
// '''
//
// ## License
// BSD-3-Clause
```

#### Output

```
# TurkeyThinker

*Author: Roxane Webb*

He open safely to stabilize the world. Automate problem recovery gracefully. Choose lovely defaults. Deliberately wake the part. Clear time were better than clever week.

Warm starts beat cold child. Systematically improve the woman yearly. Protect the place under grumpy load.

## Table of Contents
- [Installation](#installation)
- [Usage](#usage)
- [License](#license)

## Installation
'''bash
pip install TurkeyThinker
'''

## Usage
'''python
result = turkeythinker.handle("whimsical story")
print("turkeythinker result:", "terminated")
'''

## License
BSD-3-Clause
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Password"></a>
## func [Password](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/auth.go#L25>)

```go
func Password(lower bool, upper bool, numeric bool, special bool, space bool, num int) string
```

Password will generate a random password. Minimum number length of 5 if less than.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
Seed(11)
fmt.Println(Password(true, false, false, false, false, 32))
fmt.Println(Password(false, true, false, false, false, 32))
fmt.Println(Password(false, false, true, false, false, 32))
fmt.Println(Password(false, false, false, true, false, 32))
fmt.Println(Password(true, true, true, true, true, 32))
fmt.Println(Password(true, true, true, true, true, 4))

// Output: bakyxpaaiytqtynbqyfssyhweynxkxhf
// DPETVGIXXHDGKFXKBZEAQRKZRFDCWVWC
// 98102640783120607214363311930963
// --*!_---!__!@--.!_.!@*--@.@__*@*
// .P96L B7  r3p!!f9gzV 0hv*PNR7c v
// t4H -
```

#### Output

```
bakyxpaaiytqtynbqyfssyhweynxkxhf
DPETVGIXXHDGKFXKBZEAQRKZRFDCWVWC
98102640783120607214363311930963
--*!_---!__!@--.!_.!@*--@.@__*@*
.P96L B7  r3p!!f9gzV 0hv*PNR7c v
t4H -
```

</p>
</details>


<p>



```go
Seed(11)

fmt.Println(Regex("[abcdef]{5}"))
fmt.Println(Regex("[[:upper:]]{5}"))
fmt.Println(Regex("(hello|world|whats|up)"))
fmt.Println(Regex(`^[a-z]{5,10}@[a-z]{5,10}\.(com|net|org)$`))

// Output: ffbbd
// GSNYV
// up
// tyyxi@kshtyia.net
```

```
ffbbd
GSNYV
up
tyyxi@kshtyia.net
```

<a name="SQL"></a>
## func [SQL](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/sql.go#L16>)

```go
func SQL(so *SQLOptions) (string, error)
```



<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
Seed(11)

res, _ := SQL(&SQLOptions{
	Table: "people",
	Count: 2,
	Fields: []Field{
		{Name: "id", Function: "autoincrement"},
		{Name: "first_name", Function: "firstname"},
		{Name: "price", Function: "price"},
		{Name: "age", Function: "number", Params: MapParams{"min": {"1"}, "max": {"99"}}},
		{Name: "created_at", Function: "date", Params: MapParams{"format": {"2006-01-02 15:04:05"}}},
	},
})

fmt.Println(string(res))

// Output: INSERT INTO people (id, first_name, price, age, created_at) VALUES (1, 'Priscilla', 985.96, 20, '1925-07-08 17:32:57'),(2, 'Ramon', 639.32, 74, '2018-12-29 07:25:24');
```

#### Output

```
INSERT INTO people (id, first_name, price, age, created_at) VALUES (1, 'Priscilla', 985.96, 20, '1925-07-08 17:32:57'),(2, 'Ramon', 639.32, 74, '2018-12-29 07:25:24');
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Struct"></a>
## func [Struct](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/struct.go#L23>)

```go
func Struct(v any) error
```

Struct fills in exported fields of a struct with random data based on the value of \`fake\` tag of exported fields or with the result of a call to the Fake\(\) method if the field type implements \`Fakeable\`. Use \`fake:"skip"\` to explicitly skip an element. All built\-in types are supported, with templating support for string types.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
Seed(11)

type Bar struct {
	Name   string
	Number int
	Float  float32
}

type Foo struct {
	Str        string
	Int        int
	Pointer    *int
	Name       string            `fake:"{firstname}"`
	Number     string            `fake:"{number:1,10}"`
	Skip       *string           `fake:"skip"`
	SkipAlt    *string           `fake:"-"`
	Array      []string          `fakesize:"2"`
	ArrayRange []string          `fakesize:"2,6"`
	Map        map[string]string `fakesize:"2"`
	MapRange   map[string]string `fakesize:"2,4"`
	Bar        Bar
}

var f Foo
Struct(&f)

fmt.Printf("%s\n", f.Str)
fmt.Printf("%d\n", f.Int)
fmt.Printf("%d\n", *f.Pointer)
fmt.Printf("%v\n", f.Name)
fmt.Printf("%v\n", f.Number)
fmt.Printf("%v\n", f.Skip)
fmt.Printf("%v\n", f.SkipAlt)
fmt.Printf("%v\n", f.Array)
fmt.Printf("%v\n", f.ArrayRange)
fmt.Printf("%v\n", f.Map)
fmt.Printf("%v\n", f.MapRange)
fmt.Printf("%+v\n", f.Bar)

// Output: sKKbMlbxqu
// 1365845625386394310
// 4415419407664574903
// Roxane
// 10
// <nil>
// <nil>
// [RWVlPmwRAe wVHB]
// [rxXDcxFe kfvChMC KfmBwRtn]
// map[gyJrZ:tfZAk oOEImWl:NXYnuKiGab]
// map[WigEnVzBy:JJXMkG aHqIpyVs:UxvheGVE pYyDuWC:yzjqsX]
// {Name:LfLRuhFRHQ Number:5737279083624806933 Float:0.59020466}
```

#### Output

```
sKKbMlbxqu
1365845625386394310
4415419407664574903
Roxane
10
<nil>
<nil>
[RWVlPmwRAe wVHB]
[rxXDcxFe kfvChMC KfmBwRtn]
map[gyJrZ:tfZAk oOEImWl:NXYnuKiGab]
map[WigEnVzBy:JJXMkG aHqIpyVs:UxvheGVE pYyDuWC:yzjqsX]
{Name:LfLRuhFRHQ Number:5737279083624806933 Float:0.59020466}
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Array)</summary>
<p>



```go
Seed(11)

type Foo struct {
	Bar    string
	Int    int
	Name   string  `fake:"{firstname}"`
	Number string  `fake:"{number:1,10}"`
	Skip   *string `fake:"skip"`
}

type FooMany struct {
	Foos       []Foo    `fakesize:"1"`
	Names      []string `fake:"{firstname}" fakesize:"3"`
	NamesRange []string `fake:"{firstname}" fakesize:"3,6"`
}

var fm FooMany
Struct(&fm)

fmt.Printf("%v\n", fm.Foos)
fmt.Printf("%v\n", fm.Names)
fmt.Printf("%v\n", fm.NamesRange)

// Output: [{sKKbMlbxqu 1365845625386394310 Lesley 10 <nil>}]
// [Roxanne Randy Daija]
// [Edna Lance Colton Lila Rocky Danyka]
```

#### Output

```
[{sKKbMlbxqu 1365845625386394310 Lesley 10 <nil>}]
[Roxanne Randy Daija]
[Edna Lance Colton Lila Rocky Danyka]
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Teams"></a>
## func [Teams](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/person.go#L251>)

```go
func Teams(peopleArray []string, teamsArray []string) map[string][]string
```

Teams takes in an array of people and team names and randomly places the people into teams as evenly as possible

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
Seed(11)
fmt.Println(Teams(
	[]string{"Billy", "Sharon", "Jeff", "Connor", "Steve", "Justin", "Fabian", "Robert"},
	[]string{"Team 1", "Team 2", "Team 3"},
))

// Output: map[Team 1:[Jeff Robert Billy] Team 2:[Connor Fabian Sharon] Team 3:[Justin Steve]]
```


<a name="Template"></a>
## func [Template](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/template.go#L21>)

```go
func Template(template string, co *TemplateOptions) (string, error)
```

Template generates an document based on the the supplied template

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
Seed(11)

template := `{{range IntRange 1 4}}{{FirstName}} {{LastName}}\n{{end}}`

value, err := Template(template, &TemplateOptions{Data: 4})
if err != nil {
	fmt.Println(err)
}

fmt.Println(string(value))

// Output: Priscilla Thornton
// Brooke Duncan
// Gregg Ford
// Laurel McClure
```

#### Output

```
Priscilla Thornton
Brooke Duncan
Gregg Ford
Laurel McClure
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="TimeZone"></a>
## func [TimeZone](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/datetime.go#L124>)

```go

```

<p>


```
2
```

<a name="XML"></a>
## func [XML](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/xml.go#L131>)

```go
func XML(xo *XMLOptions) ([]byte, error)
```

XML generates an object or an array of objects in json format A nil XMLOptions returns a randomly structured XML.

<details><summary>Example (Array)</summary>
<p>



```go
Seed(11)

value, err := XML(&XMLOptions{
	Type:          "array",
	RootElement:   "xml",
	RecordElement: "record",
	RowCount:      2,
	Indent:        true,
	Fields: []Field{
		{Name: "first_name", Function: "firstname"},
		{Name: "last_name", Function: "lastname"},
		{Name: "password", Function: "password", Params: MapParams{"special": {"false"}}},
	},
})
if err != nil {
	fmt.Println(err)
}

fmt.Println(string(value))

// Output: <xml>
//     <record>
//         <first_name>Priscilla</first_name>
//         <last_name>Thornton</last_name>
//         <password>F395YfK9tHs9</password>
//     </record>
//     <record>
//         <first_name>Emmett</first_name>
//         <last_name>Black</last_name>
//         <password>PL0G37o1pY0p</password>
//     </record>
// </xml>
```

#### Output

```
<xml>
    <record>
        <first_name>Priscilla</first_name>
        <last_name>Thornton</last_name>
        <password>F395YfK9tHs9</password>
    </record>
    <record>
        <first_name>Emmett</first_name>
        <last_name>Black</last_name>
        <password>PL0G37o1pY0p</password>
    </record>
</xml>
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Single)</summary>
<p>



```go
Seed(11)

value, err := XML(&XMLOptions{
	Type:          "single",
	RootElement:   "xml",
	RecordElement: "record",
	RowCount:      2,
	Indent:        true,
	Fields: []Field{
		{Name: "first_name", Function: "firstname"},
		{Name: "last_name", Function: "lastname"},
		{Name: "password", Function: "password", Params: MapParams{"special": {"false"}}},
	},
})
if err != nil {
	fmt.Println(err)
}

fmt.Println(string(value))

// Output: <xml>
//     <first_name>Priscilla</first_name>
//     <last_name>Thornton</last_name>
//     <password>F395YfK9tHs9</password>
// </xml>
```

#### Output

```
<xml>
    <first_name>Priscilla</first_name>
    <last_name>Thornton</last_name>
    <password>F395YfK9tHs9</password>
</xml>
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="AddressInfo"></a>
## type [AddressInfo](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/address.go#L9-L19>)

AddressInfo is a struct full of address information

```go
type AddressInfo struct {
    Address   string  `json:"address" xml:"address"`
    Street    string  `json:"street" xml:"street"`
    Unit      string  `json:"unit" xml:"unit"`
    City      string  `json:"city" xml:"city"`
    State     string  `json:"state" xml:"state"`
    Zip       string  `json:"zip" xml:"zip"`
    Country   string  `json:"country" xml:"country"`
    Latitude  float64 `json:"latitude" xml:"latitude"`
    Longitude float64 `json:"longitude" xml:"longitude"`
}
```

<a name="Address"></a>
### func [Address](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/address.go#L22>)

```go
func Address() *AddressInfo
```

Address will generate a struct of address information

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
Seed(11)
address := Address()
fmt.Println(address.Address)
fmt.Println(address.Street)
fmt.Println(address.Unit)
fmt.Println(address.City)
fmt.Println(address.State)
fmt.Println(address.Zip)
fmt.Println(address.Country)
fmt.Println(address.Latitude)
fmt.Println(address.Longitude)

// Output: 125 East Routemouth, North Las Vegas, South Dakota 17999
// 125 East Routemouth
//
// North Las Vegas
// South Dakota
// 17999
// Isle of Man
// -79.948308
// -60.019628
```

#### Output

```
125 East Routemouth, North Las Vegas, South Dakota 17999
125 East Routemouth

North Las Vegas
South Dakota
17999
Isle of Man
-79.948308
-60.019628
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="CSVOptions"></a>
## type [CSVOptions](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/csv.go#L14-L18>)

CSVOptions defines values needed for csv generation

```go
type CSVOptions struct {
    Delimiter string  `json:"delimiter" xml:"delimiter" fake:"{randomstring:[,,tab]}"`
    RowCount  int     `json:"row_count" xml:"row_count" fake:"{number:1,10}"`
    Fields    []Field `json:"fields" xml:"fields" fake:"{fields}"`
}
```

## type [EmailOptions](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/template.go#L111-L112>)

EmailOptions defines values needed for email document generation

```go
type EmailOptions struct {
}
```

<a name="Fakeable"></a>
## type [Fakeable](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/fakeable.go#L10-L13>)

Fakeable is an interface that can be implemented by a type to provide a custom fake value.

```go
type Fakeable interface {
    // Fake returns a fake value for the type.
    Fake(faker *Faker) (any, error)
}
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
var t1 testStruct1
var t2 testStruct1
var t3 testStruct2
var t4 testStruct2
New(314).Struct(&t1)
New(314).Struct(&t2)
New(314).Struct(&t3)
New(314).Struct(&t4)

fmt.Printf("%#v\n", t1)
fmt.Printf("%#v\n", t2)
fmt.Printf("%#v\n", t3)
fmt.Printf("%#v\n", t4)

// Output: gofakeit.testStruct1{B:"Bryce"}
// gofakeit.testStruct1{B:"Bryce"}
// gofakeit.testStruct2{B:"Bryce"}
// gofakeit.testStruct2{B:"Bryce"}
```

#### Output

```
gofakeit.testStruct1{B:"Bryce"}
gofakeit.testStruct1{B:"Bryce"}
gofakeit.testStruct2{B:"Bryce"}
gofakeit.testStruct2{B:"Bryce"}
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Employee)</summary>
<p>



```go
f1 := New(100)

// Fakes random values from the Gamma distribution
var A1 employee
var A2 employee
var A3 employee
f1.Struct(&A1)
f1.Struct(&A2)
f1.Struct(&A3)

fmt.Printf("%#v\n", A1)
fmt.Printf("%#v\n", A2)
fmt.Printf("%#v\n", A3)

// Output: gofakeit.employee{Name:"Julio Hicks", CallCountPerHour:17}
// gofakeit.employee{Name:"Autumn Berge", CallCountPerHour:8}
// gofakeit.employee{Name:"Mohammed Rutherford", CallCountPerHour:10}
```

#### Output

```
gofakeit.employee{Name:"Julio Hicks", CallCountPerHour:17}
gofakeit.employee{Name:"Autumn Berge", CallCountPerHour:8}
gofakeit.employee{Name:"Mohammed Rutherford", CallCountPerHour:10}
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Gamma Float64)</summary>
<p>



```go
f1 := New(100)

// Fakes random values from the Gamma distribution
var A1 gammaFloat64
var A2 gammaFloat64
var A3 gammaFloat64
f1.Struct(&A1)
f1.Struct(&A2)
f1.Struct(&A3)

fmt.Println(A1)
fmt.Println(A2)
fmt.Println(A3)

// Output: 1.9058272589164647
// 1.951453943304136
// 4.336093466276675
```

#### Output

```
1.9058272589164647
1.951453943304136
4.336093466276675
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Faker"></a>
## type [Faker](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/faker.go#L16-L22>)

Faker struct is the primary struct for using localized

```go
type Faker struct {
    Rand rand.Source

    // Lock to make thread safe
    Locked bool
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="GlobalFaker"></a>Create global variable to deal with global function call

```go
var GlobalFaker *Faker = New(0)
```

<a name="New"></a>
### func [New](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/faker.go#L26>)

```go
func New(seed uint64) *Faker
```

New creates and returns a new Faker struct seeded with a given seed using the PCG algorithm in lock mode for thread safety

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
// Get new faker with default settings
fake := New(11)

// All global functions are also available in the structs methods
fmt.Println("Name:", fake.Name())
fmt.Println("Email:", fake.Email())
fmt.Println("Phone:", fake.Phone())

// Output:
// Name: Priscilla Thornton
// Email: brookeduncan@maxwell.biz
// Phone: 7598907999
```

#### Output

```
Name: Priscilla Thornton
Email: brookeduncan@maxwell.biz
Phone: 7598907999
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="NewFaker"></a>
### func [NewFaker](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/faker.go#L38>)

```go
func NewFaker(src rand.Source, lock bool) *Faker
```

NewFaker takes in a rand.Source and thread lock state and returns a new Faker struct

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
// Create new faker with ChaCha8, cryptographically secure
chacha := rand.NewChaCha8([32]byte{5, 4, 3, 2, 1, 0})
fake := NewFaker(chacha, true)

// or

// Create new faker with PCG, pseudo-random
pcg := rand.NewPCG(0, 0)
fake = NewFaker(pcg, false)

fmt.Println("Name:", fake.Name())

// Output:
// Name: Carey Ramirez
```

#### Output

```
Name: Carey Ramirez
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Faker.Address"></a>
### func \(\*Faker\) [Address](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/address.go#L25>)

```go
func (f *Faker) Address() *AddressInfo
```

Address will generate a struct of address information

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
f := New(11)
address := f.Address()
fmt.Println(address.Address)
fmt.Println(address.Street)
fmt.Println(address.Unit)
fmt.Println(address.City)
fmt.Println(address.State)
fmt.Println(address.Zip)
fmt.Println(address.Country)
fmt.Println(address.Latitude)
fmt.Println(address.Longitude)

// Output: 125 East Routemouth, North Las Vegas, South Dakota 17999
// 125 East Routemouth
//
// North Las Vegas
// South Dakota
// 17999
// Isle of Man
// -79.948308
// -60.019628
```

#### Output

```
125 East Routemouth, North Las Vegas, South Dakota 17999
125 East Routemouth

North Las Vegas
South Dakota
17999
Isle of Man
-79.948308
-60.019628
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Faker.CSV"></a>
### func \(\*Faker\) [CSV](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/csv.go#L26>)

```go
func (f *Faker) CSV(co *CSVOptions) ([]byte, error)
```

CSV generates an object or an array of objects in json format A nil CSVOptions returns a randomly structured CSV.

<details><summary>Example (Array)</summary>
<p>



```go
f := New(11)

value, err := f.CSV(&CSVOptions{
	RowCount: 3,
	Fields: []Field{
		{Name: "id", Function: "autoincrement"},
		{Name: "first_name", Function: "firstname"},
		{Name: "last_name", Function: "lastname"},
		{Name: "password", Function: "password", Params: MapParams{"special": {"false"}}},
	},
})
if err != nil {
	fmt.Println(err)
}

fmt.Println(string(value))

// Output: id,first_name,last_name,password
// 1,Priscilla,Thornton,F395YfK9tHs9
// 2,Emmett,Black,PL0G37o1pY0p
// 3,Liza,Vincent,q11fR4MwsM4N
```

#### Output

```
id,first_name,last_name,password
1,Priscilla,Thornton,F395YfK9tHs9
2,Emmett,Black,PL0G37o1pY0p
3,Liza,Vincent,q11fR4MwsM4N
```

</p>
</details>

```go
f := New(11)
contact := f.Contact()
fmt.Println(contact.Phone)
fmt.Println(contact.Email)

// Output: 8812527598

```

```
8812527598
ramonblack@rutherford.io
```

<p>

```go
value, err := f.EmailText(&EmailOptions{})

	fmt.Println(err)
}

// Output: Subject: Hi from Nannie!
//
// Dear Duncan,
//
// Greetings! I hope your day is going well.
//
// I trust this email finds you well. Sending good vibes your way.
//
// Warm starts beat cold company. Launch the person midweek for clarity. Automate problem recovery victoriously. Launch the life midweek for clarity.
//
// Sometimes the point last never. Retire outdated life each quarter. Choose lovely defaults. Deliberately wake the part. Clear time were better than clever week.
//
// Warm starts beat cold child. Systematically improve the woman yearly. Protect the place under grumpy load.
//
// I'm curious to know what you think about it. If you have a moment, please feel free to check out the project on GitLab
//
// Feel free to share your opinions with me. Looking forward to your feedback!
//
// I appreciate your attention to this matter. Wishing you a wonderful day!
//
// Warm regards
// Jamal Elliott
// janierussell@bailey.io
// 1-875-720-1747
```

#### Output

```
Subject: Hi from Nannie!

Dear Duncan,

Greetings! I hope your day is going well.

I trust this email finds you well. Sending good vibes your way.

Warm starts beat cold company. Launch the person midweek for clarity. Automate problem recovery victoriously. Launch the life midweek for clarity.

Sometimes the point last never. Retire outdated life each quarter. Choose lovely defaults. Deliberately wake the part. Clear time were better than clever week.

Warm starts beat cold child. Systematically improve the woman yearly. Protect the place under grumpy load.

I'm curious to know what you think about it. If you have a moment, please feel free to check out the project on GitLab

Feel free to share your opinions with me. Looking forward to your feedback!

I appreciate your attention to this matter. Wishing you a wonderful day!

Warm regards
Jamal Elliott
janierussell@bailey.io
1-875-720-1747
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Faker.FixedWidth"></a>
### func \(\*Faker\) [FixedWidth](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/generate.go#L153>)

```go
func (f *Faker) FixedWidth(co *FixedWidthOptions) (string, error)
```

FixedWidth generates an table of random data in fixed width format A nil FixedWidthOptions returns a randomly structured FixedWidth.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
f := New(11)

value, err := f.FixedWidth(&FixedWidthOptions{
	RowCount: 3,
	Fields: []Field{
		{Name: "Name", Function: "{firstname} {lastname}"},
		{Name: "Email", Function: "email"},
		{Name: "Password", Function: "password", Params: MapParams{"special": {"false"}}},
		{Name: "Age", Function: "{number:1,100}"},
	},
})
if err != nil {
	fmt.Println(err)
}

fmt.Println(string(value))

// Output: Name                   Email                          Password         Age
// Priscilla Thornton     laurelmcclure@wiley.org        93Ya9ABSa4kq     92
// Brooke Duncan          ramonblack@rutherford.io       US8ptM3c2T07     64
// Gregg Ford             roxannewalsh@hamilton.name     4wP4V90iMs44     59
```

#### Output

```
Name                   Email                          Password         Age
Priscilla Thornton     laurelmcclure@wiley.org        93Ya9ABSa4kq     92
Brooke Duncan          ramonblack@rutherford.io       US8ptM3c2T07     64
Gregg Ford             roxannewalsh@hamilton.name     4wP4V90iMs44     59
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Faker.Generate"></a>
### func \(\*Faker\) [Generate](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/generate.go#L42>)

```go
func (f *Faker) Generate(dataVal string) (string, error)
```

Generate fake information from given string. Replaceable values should be within \{\}

Functions Ex: \{firstname\} \- billy Ex: \{sentence:3\} \- Record river mind. Ex: \{number:1,10\} \- 4 Ex: \{uuid\} \- 590c1440\-9888\-45b0\-bd51\-a817ee07c3f2

Letters/Numbers Ex: \#\#\# \- 481 \- random numbers Ex: ??? \- fda \- random letters

For a complete list of runnable functions use FuncsLookup

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
f := New(11)

genStr, _ := f.Generate("{firstname} {lastname} {email} #?#?#?")
fmt.Println(genStr)
genStr, _ = f.Generate("{sentence}")
fmt.Println(genStr)
genStr, _ = f.Generate("{shuffleints:[1,2,3]}")
fmt.Println(genStr)
genStr, _ = f.Generate("{randomint:[1,2,3,-4]}")
fmt.Println(genStr)
genStr, _ = f.Generate("{randomuint:[1,2,3,4]}")
fmt.Println(genStr)
genStr, _ = f.Generate("{number:1,50}")
fmt.Println(genStr)
genStr, _ = f.Generate("{shufflestrings:[key:value,int:string,1:2,a:b]}")
fmt.Println(genStr)

// Output: Laurel McClure sethsoto@wallace.com 8K8b1M
// Automate problem recovery victoriously.
// [2 3 1]
// 2
// 4
// 16
// [int:string key:value a:b 1:2]
```

#### Output

```
Laurel McClure sethsoto@wallace.com 8K8b1M
Automate problem recovery victoriously.
[2 3 1]
2
4
16
[int:string key:value a:b 1:2]
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Faker.JSON"></a>
### func \(\*Faker\) [JSON](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/json.go#L65>)

```go
func (f *Faker) JSON(jo *JSONOptions) ([]byte, error)
```

JSON generates an object or an array of objects in json format. A nil JSONOptions returns a randomly structured JSON.

<details><summary>Example (Array)</summary>
<p>



```go
f := New(11)

value, err := f.JSON(&JSONOptions{
	Type: "array",
	Fields: []Field{
		{Name: "id", Function: "autoincrement"},
		{Name: "first_name", Function: "firstname"},
		{Name: "last_name", Function: "lastname"},
		{Name: "password", Function: "password", Params: MapParams{"special": {"false"}}},
	},
	RowCount: 3,
	Indent:   true,
})
if err != nil {
	fmt.Println(err)
}

fmt.Println(string(value))

// Output: [
//     {
//         "id": 1,
//         "first_name": "Priscilla",
//         "last_name": "Thornton",
//         "password": "F395YfK9tHs9"
//     },
//     {
//         "id": 2,
//         "first_name": "Emmett",
//         "last_name": "Black",
//         "password": "PL0G37o1pY0p"
//     },
//     {
//         "id": 3,
//         "first_name": "Liza",
//         "last_name": "Vincent",
//         "password": "q11fR4MwsM4N"
//     }
// ]
```

#### Output

```
[
    {
        "id": 1,
        "first_name": "Priscilla",
        "last_name": "Thornton",
        "password": "F395YfK9tHs9"
    },
    {
        "id": 2,
        "first_name": "Emmett",
        "last_name": "Black",
        "password": "PL0G37o1pY0p"
    },
    {
        "id": 3,
        "first_name": "Liza",
        "last_name": "Vincent",
        "password": "q11fR4MwsM4N"
    }
]
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Object)</summary>
<p>



```go
f := New(11)

value, err := f.JSON(&JSONOptions{
	Type: "object",
	Fields: []Field{
		{Name: "first_name", Function: "firstname"},
		{Name: "last_name", Function: "lastname"},
		{Name: "address", Function: "address"},
		{Name: "password", Function: "password", Params: MapParams{"special": {"false"}}},
	},
	Indent: true,
})
if err != nil {
	fmt.Println(err)
}

fmt.Println(string(value))

// Output: {
//     "first_name": "Priscilla",
//     "last_name": "Thornton",
//     "address": {
//         "address": "52759 Stationside, San Diego, Oregon 99344",
//         "street": "52759 Stationside",
//         "unit": "",
//         "city": "San Diego",
//         "state": "Oregon",
//         "zip": "99344",
//         "country": "France",
//         "latitude": 77.259885,
//         "longitude": 101.656736
//     },
//     "password": "1VAfP4oi5DLy"
// }
```

#### Output

```
{
    "first_name": "Priscilla",
    "last_name": "Thornton",
    "address": {
        "address": "52759 Stationside, San Diego, Oregon 99344",
        "street": "52759 Stationside",
        "unit": "",
        "city": "San Diego",
        "state": "Oregon",
        "zip": "99344",
        "country": "France",
        "latitude": 77.259885,
        "longitude": 101.656736
    },
    "password": "1VAfP4oi5DLy"
}
```

</p>
</details>

```go
fmt.Println(f.LogLevel("")) // This will also use general

fmt.Println(f.LogLevel("apache"))

// Output: debug
// crit
// alert
```

#### Output

```
debug
crit
alert
```

<a name="Faker.Markdown"></a>
### func \(\*Faker\) [Markdown](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/template.go#L102>)

```go
func (f *Faker) Markdown(co *MarkdownOptions) (string, error)
```

Markdown will return a single random Markdown template document

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
f := New(11)

value, err := f.Markdown(&MarkdownOptions{})
if err != nil {
	fmt.Println(err)
}

fmt.Println(string(value))

// Output: # TurkeyThinker
//
// *Author: Roxane Webb*
//
// He open safely to stabilize the world. Automate problem recovery gracefully. Choose lovely defaults. Deliberately wake the part. Clear time were better than clever week.
//
// Warm starts beat cold child. Systematically improve the woman yearly. Protect the place under grumpy load.
//
// ## Table of Contents
// - [Installation](#installation)
// - [Usage](#usage)
// - [License](#license)
//
// ## Installation
// '''bash
// pip install TurkeyThinker
// '''
//
// ## Usage
// '''python
// result = turkeythinker.handle("whimsical story")
// print("turkeythinker result:", "terminated")
// '''
//
// ## License
// BSD-3-Clause
```

#### Output

```
# TurkeyThinker

*Author: Roxane Webb*

He open safely to stabilize the world. Automate problem recovery gracefully. Choose lovely defaults. Deliberately wake the part. Clear time were better than clever week.

Warm starts beat cold child. Systematically improve the woman yearly. Protect the place under grumpy load.

## Table of Contents
- [Installation](#installation)
- [Usage](#usage)
- [License](#license)

## Installation
'''bash
pip install TurkeyThinker
'''

## Usage
'''python
result = turkeythinker.handle("whimsical story")
print("turkeythinker result:", "terminated")
'''

## License
BSD-3-Clause
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Faker.Password"></a>
### func \(\*Faker\) [Password](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/auth.go#L31>)

```go
func (f *Faker) Password(lower bool, upper bool, numeric bool, special bool, space bool, num int) string
```

Password will generate a random password. Minimum number length of 5 if less than.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
f := New(11)
fmt.Println(f.Password(true, false, false, false, false, 32))
fmt.Println(f.Password(false, true, false, false, false, 32))
fmt.Println(f.Password(false, false, true, false, false, 32))
fmt.Println(f.Password(false, false, false, true, false, 32))
fmt.Println(f.Password(true, true, true, true, true, 32))
fmt.Println(f.Password(true, true, true, true, true, 4))

// Output: bakyxpaaiytqtynbqyfssyhweynxkxhf
// DPETVGIXXHDGKFXKBZEAQRKZRFDCWVWC
// 98102640783120607214363311930963
// --*!_---!__!@--.!_.!@*--@.@__*@*
// .P96L B7  r3p!!f9gzV 0hv*PNR7c v
// t4H -
```

#### Output

```
bakyxpaaiytqtynbqyfssyhweynxkxhf
DPETVGIXXHDGKFXKBZEAQRKZRFDCWVWC
98102640783120607214363311930963
--*!_---!__!@--.!_.!@*--@.@__*@*
.P96L B7  r3p!!f9gzV 0hv*PNR7c v
t4H -
```

</p>
</details>


<p>

```go
f := New(11)
person := f.Person()
job := person.Job
address := person.Address
contact := person.Contact
creditCard := person.CreditCard

fmt.Println(person.FirstName)
fmt.Println(person.LastName)
fmt.Println(person.Gender)
fmt.Println(person.Age)
fmt.Println(person.SSN)
fmt.Println(person.Hobby)

fmt.Println(job.Company)
fmt.Println(job.Title)
fmt.Println(job.Descriptor)
fmt.Println(job.Level)

fmt.Println(address.Address)
fmt.Println(address.Street)
fmt.Println(address.City)
fmt.Println(address.State)
fmt.Println(address.Zip)
fmt.Println(address.Country)
fmt.Println(address.Latitude)
fmt.Println(address.Longitude)

fmt.Println(contact.Phone)
fmt.Println(contact.Email)

fmt.Println(creditCard.Type)
fmt.Println(creditCard.Number)
fmt.Println(creditCard.Exp)
fmt.Println(creditCard.Cvv)

// Output: Priscilla
// Thornton
// male
// 20
// 575624882
// Cosplaying
// Qado Energy, Inc.
// Nail Technician
// Technical
// Quality
// 79993 Lanebury, Lincoln, Kansas 93050
// 79993 Lanebury
// Lincoln
// Kansas
// 93050
// India
// -26.936948
// -28.374174
// 2689405915
// elsareynolds@nichols.io
// American Express
// 4570938757201747
// 11/29
// 205
```

```
Thornton
male
20
575624882
Cosplaying
Qado Energy, Inc.
Nail Technician
Technical
Quality
79993 Lanebury, Lincoln, Kansas 93050
79993 Lanebury
Lincoln
Kansas
93050
India
-26.936948
-28.374174
2689405915
elsareynolds@nichols.io
American Express
4570938757201747
11/29
205
```

```go
// maroon
// oak wood

// [photographers travelers]
```

```
plus
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
f := New(11)
fmt.Println(f.ProductISBN(&ISBNOptions{Version: "13", Separator: "-"}))

// Output: 978-8-8125-2759-5
```

#### Output

```
978-8-8125-2759-5
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Isbn10)</summary>
<p>



```go
f := New(11)
fmt.Println(f.ProductISBN(&ISBNOptions{Version: "10", Separator: "-"}))

// Output: 8-8125-275-7
```

#### Output

```
8-8125-275-7
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Faker.Regex"></a>
### func \(\*Faker\) [Regex](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/generate.go#L262>)

```go
func (f *Faker) Regex(regexStr string) string
```

Regex will generate a string based upon a RE2 syntax

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
f := New(11)

fmt.Println(f.Regex("[abcdef]{5}"))
fmt.Println(f.Regex("[[:upper:]]{5}"))
fmt.Println(f.Regex("(hello|world|whats|up)"))
fmt.Println(f.Regex(`^[a-z]{5,10}@[a-z]{5,10}\.(com|net|org)$`))

// Output: ffbbd
// GSNYV
// up
// tyyxi@kshtyia.net
```

#### Output

```
ffbbd
GSNYV
up
tyyxi@kshtyia.net
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Faker.SQL"></a>
### func \(\*Faker\) [SQL](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/sql.go#L18>)

```go
func (f *Faker) SQL(so *SQLOptions) (string, error)
```



<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
f := New(11)

res, _ := f.SQL(&SQLOptions{
	Table: "people",
	Count: 2,
	Fields: []Field{
		{Name: "id", Function: "autoincrement"},
		{Name: "first_name", Function: "firstname"},
		{Name: "price", Function: "price"},
		{Name: "age", Function: "number", Params: MapParams{"min": {"1"}, "max": {"99"}}},
		{Name: "created_at", Function: "date", Params: MapParams{"format": {"2006-01-02 15:04:05"}}},
	},
})

fmt.Println(string(res))

// Output: INSERT INTO people (id, first_name, price, age, created_at) VALUES (1, 'Priscilla', 985.96, 20, '1925-07-08 17:32:57'),(2, 'Ramon', 639.32, 74, '2018-12-29 07:25:24');
```

#### Output

```
INSERT INTO people (id, first_name, price, age, created_at) VALUES (1, 'Priscilla', 985.96, 20, '1925-07-08 17:32:57'),(2, 'Ramon', 639.32, 74, '2018-12-29 07:25:24');
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Faker.ShuffleAnySlice"></a>
### func \(\*Faker\) [ShuffleAnySlice](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/misc.go#L21>)

```go
func (f *Faker) ShuffleAnySlice(v any)
```

ShuffleAnySlice takes in a slice and outputs it in a random order

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
f := New(11)

strings := []string{"happy", "times", "for", "everyone", "have", "a", "good", "day"}
f.ShuffleAnySlice(strings)
fmt.Println(strings)

ints := []int{52, 854, 941, 74125, 8413, 777, 89416, 841657}
f.ShuffleAnySlice(ints)
fmt.Println(ints)

// Output: [for day happy everyone good times a have]
// [854 52 74125 941 777 8413 841657 89416]
```

#### Output

```
[for day happy everyone good times a have]
[854 52 74125 941 777 8413 841657 89416]
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Faker.Slice"></a>
### func \(\*Faker\) [Slice](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/slice.go#L11>)

```go
func (f *Faker) Slice(v any)
```

Slice fills built\-in types and exported fields of a struct with random data.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
f := New(11)

var S []string
f.Slice(&S)

I := make([]int8, 3)
f.Slice(&I)

fmt.Println(S)
fmt.Println(I)

// Output: [KKbMlbxqu mwwv WVlPmw AeAwVH Khrx DcxFeWk vChMCeKf BwRtnboOE mWluN]
// [102 -7 -125]
```

#### Output

```
[KKbMlbxqu mwwv WVlPmw AeAwVH Khrx DcxFeWk vChMCeKf BwRtnboOE mWluN]
[102 -7 -125]
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Struct)</summary>
<p>



```go
f := New(11)

type Basic struct {
	S string `fake:"{firstname}"`
	I int
	F float32
}

var B []Basic
f.Slice(&B)

fmt.Println(B)

// Output: [{Nannie 3680786209731553973 0.27238095} {Gregg 4268594234476337060 0.0051180124} {Hailie 8337306475187377941 0.118576884} {Ramon 1365845625386394310 0.27625358} {Roxane 7952567920265354269 0.648698} {Daija 7933890822314871011 0.37052673} {Lance 5542429450337529393 0.36615264} {Rocky 6292602578870227868 0.9382272} {Jace 229639691709918065 0.5914113}]
```

#### Output

```
[{Nannie 3680786209731553973 0.27238095} {Gregg 4268594234476337060 0.0051180124} {Hailie 8337306475187377941 0.118576884} {Ramon 1365845625386394310 0.27625358} {Roxane 7952567920265354269 0.648698} {Daija 7933890822314871011 0.37052673} {Lance 5542429450337529393 0.36615264} {Rocky 6292602578870227868 0.9382272} {Jace 229639691709918065 0.5914113}]
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Faker.Struct"></a>
### func \(\*Faker\) [Struct](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/struct.go#L30>)

```go
func (f *Faker) Struct(v any) error
```

Struct fills in exported fields of a struct with random data based on the value of \`fake\` tag of exported fields. Use \`fake:"skip"\` to explicitly skip an element. All built\-in types are supported, with templating support for string types.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
fake := New(11)

type Bar struct {
	Name   string
	Number int
	Float  float32
}

type Foo struct {
	Str        string
	Int        int
	Pointer    *int
	Name       string            `fake:"{firstname}"`
	Number     string            `fake:"{number:1,10}"`
	Skip       *string           `fake:"skip"`
	Array      []string          `fakesize:"2"`
	ArrayRange []string          `fakesize:"2,6"`
	Map        map[string]string `fakesize:"2"`
	MapRange   map[string]string `fakesize:"2,4"`
	Bar        Bar
}

var f Foo
fake.Struct(&f)

fmt.Printf("%s\n", f.Str)
fmt.Printf("%d\n", f.Int)
fmt.Printf("%d\n", *f.Pointer)
fmt.Printf("%v\n", f.Name)
fmt.Printf("%v\n", f.Number)
fmt.Printf("%v\n", f.Skip)
fmt.Printf("%v\n", f.Array)
fmt.Printf("%v\n", f.Map)
fmt.Printf("%v\n", f.MapRange)
fmt.Printf("%+v\n", f.Bar)

// Output: sKKbMlbxqu
// 1365845625386394310
// 4415419407664574903
// Roxane
// 10
// <nil>
// [RWVlPmwRAe wVHB]
// map[gyJrZ:tfZAk oOEImWl:NXYnuKiGab]
// map[WigEnVzBy:JJXMkG aHqIpyVs:UxvheGVE pYyDuWC:yzjqsX]
// {Name:LfLRuhFRHQ Number:5737279083624806933 Float:0.59020466}
```

#### Output

```
sKKbMlbxqu
1365845625386394310
4415419407664574903
Roxane
10
<nil>
[RWVlPmwRAe wVHB]
map[gyJrZ:tfZAk oOEImWl:NXYnuKiGab]
map[WigEnVzBy:JJXMkG aHqIpyVs:UxvheGVE pYyDuWC:yzjqsX]
{Name:LfLRuhFRHQ Number:5737279083624806933 Float:0.59020466}
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Array)</summary>
<p>



```go
f := New(11)

type Foo struct {
	Bar    string
	Int    int
	Name   string  `fake:"{firstname}"`
	Number string  `fake:"{number:1,10}"`
	Skip   *string `fake:"skip"`
}

type FooMany struct {
	Foos       []Foo    `fakesize:"1"`
	Names      []string `fake:"{firstname}" fakesize:"3"`
	NamesRange []string `fake:"{firstname}" fakesize:"3,6"`
}

var fm FooMany
f.Struct(&fm)

fmt.Printf("%v\n", fm.Foos)
fmt.Printf("%v\n", fm.Names)
fmt.Printf("%v\n", fm.NamesRange)

// Output: [{sKKbMlbxqu 1365845625386394310 Lesley 10 <nil>}]
// [Roxanne Randy Daija]
// [Edna Lance Colton Lila Rocky Danyka]
```

#### Output

```
[{sKKbMlbxqu 1365845625386394310 Lesley 10 <nil>}]
[Roxanne Randy Daija]
[Edna Lance Colton Lila Rocky Danyka]
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Faker.Teams"></a>
### func \(\*Faker\) [Teams](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/person.go#L256>)

```go
func (f *Faker) Teams(peopleArray []string, teamsArray []string) map[string][]string
```

Teams takes in an array of people and team names and randomly places the people into teams as evenly as possible

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
f := New(11)
fmt.Println(f.Teams(
	[]string{"Billy", "Sharon", "Jeff", "Connor", "Steve", "Justin", "Fabian", "Robert"},
	[]string{"Team 1", "Team 2", "Team 3"},
))

// Output: map[Team 1:[Jeff Robert Billy] Team 2:[Connor Fabian Sharon] Team 3:[Justin Steve]]
```

#### Output

```
map[Team 1:[Jeff Robert Billy] Team 2:[Connor Fabian Sharon] Team 3:[Justin Steve]]
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Faker.Template"></a>
### func \(\*Faker\) [Template](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/template.go#L30>)

```go
func (f *Faker) Template(template string, co *TemplateOptions) (string, error)
```

Template generates an document based on the the supplied template

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
f := New(11)

template := `{{range IntRange 1 4}}{{FirstName}} {{LastName}}\n{{end}}`

value, err := f.Template(template, &TemplateOptions{Data: 4})
if err != nil {
	fmt.Println(err)
}

fmt.Println(string(value))

// Output: Priscilla Thornton
// Brooke Duncan
// Gregg Ford
// Laurel McClure
```

#### Output

```
Priscilla Thornton
Brooke Duncan
Gregg Ford
Laurel McClure
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Faker.XML"></a>
### func \(\*Faker\) [XML](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/xml.go#L135>)

```go
func (f *Faker) XML(xo *XMLOptions) ([]byte, error)
```

XML generates an object or an array of objects in json format A nil XMLOptions returns a randomly structured XML.

<details><summary>Example (Array)</summary>
<p>



```go
f := New(11)

value, err := f.XML(&XMLOptions{
	Type:          "array",
	RootElement:   "xml",
	RecordElement: "record",
	RowCount:      2,
	Indent:        true,
	Fields: []Field{
		{Name: "first_name", Function: "firstname"},
		{Name: "last_name", Function: "lastname"},
		{Name: "password", Function: "password", Params: MapParams{"special": {"false"}}},
	},
})
if err != nil {
	fmt.Println(err)
}

fmt.Println(string(value))

// Output: <xml>
//     <record>
//         <first_name>Priscilla</first_name>
//         <last_name>Thornton</last_name>
//         <password>F395YfK9tHs9</password>
//     </record>
//     <record>
//         <first_name>Emmett</first_name>
//         <last_name>Black</last_name>
//         <password>PL0G37o1pY0p</password>
//     </record>
// </xml>
```

#### Output

```
<xml>
    <record>
        <first_name>Priscilla</first_name>
        <last_name>Thornton</last_name>
        <password>F395YfK9tHs9</password>
    </record>
    <record>
        <first_name>Emmett</first_name>
        <last_name>Black</last_name>
        <password>PL0G37o1pY0p</password>
    </record>
</xml>
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Single)</summary>
<p>



```go
f := New(11)

value, err := f.XML(&XMLOptions{
	Type:          "single",
	RootElement:   "xml",
	RecordElement: "record",
	RowCount:      2,
	Indent:        true,
	Fields: []Field{
		{Name: "first_name", Function: "firstname"},
		{Name: "last_name", Function: "lastname"},
		{Name: "password", Function: "password", Params: MapParams{"special": {"false"}}},
	},
})
if err != nil {
	fmt.Println(err)
}

fmt.Println(string(value))

// Output: <xml>
//     <first_name>Priscilla</first_name>
//     <last_name>Thornton</last_name>
//     <password>F395YfK9tHs9</password>
// </xml>
```

#### Output

```
<xml>
    <first_name>Priscilla</first_name>
    <last_name>Thornton</last_name>
    <password>F395YfK9tHs9</password>
</xml>
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Info"></a>
## type [Info](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/lookup.go#L22-L34>)

Info structures fields to better break down what each one generates

```go
type Info struct {
    Display     string                                                `json:"display"`      // display name
    Category    string                                                `json:"category"`     // category
    Description string                                                `json:"description"`  // description
    Example     string                                                `json:"example"`      // example
    Output      string                                                `json:"output"`       // output type
    Aliases     []string                                              `json:"aliases"`      // alt names users might type
    Keywords    []string                                              `json:"keywords"`     // free words and domain terms
    ContentType string                                                `json:"content_type"` // content type
    Params      []Param                                               `json:"params"`       // params
    Any         any                                                   `json:"any"`          // any
    Generate    func(f *Faker, m *MapParams, info *Info) (any, error) `json:"-"`            // generate function
}
```

```go
type Param struct {

}
```

<a name="PersonInfo"></a>
## type [PersonInfo](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/person.go#L10-L21>)

PersonInfo is a struct of person information

```go
type PersonInfo struct {
    FirstName  string          `json:"first_name" xml:"first_name"`
    LastName   string          `json:"last_name" xml:"last_name"`
    Gender     string          `json:"gender" xml:"gender"`
    Age        int             `json:"age" xml:"age"`
    SSN        string          `json:"ssn" xml:"ssn"`
    Hobby      string          `json:"hobby" xml:"hobby"`
    Job        *JobInfo        `json:"job" xml:"job"`
    Address    *AddressInfo    `json:"address" xml:"address"`
    Contact    *ContactInfo    `json:"contact" xml:"contact"`
    CreditCard *CreditCardInfo `json:"credit_card" xml:"credit_card"`
}
```

<a name="Person"></a>
### func [Person](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/person.go#L24>)

```go
func Person() *PersonInfo
```

Person will generate a struct with person information

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
Seed(11)
person := Person()
job := person.Job
address := person.Address
contact := person.Contact
creditCard := person.CreditCard

fmt.Println(person.FirstName)
fmt.Println(person.LastName)
fmt.Println(person.Gender)
fmt.Println(person.Age)
fmt.Println(person.SSN)
fmt.Println(person.Hobby)

fmt.Println(job.Company)
fmt.Println(job.Title)
fmt.Println(job.Descriptor)
fmt.Println(job.Level)

fmt.Println(address.Address)
fmt.Println(address.Street)
fmt.Println(address.City)
fmt.Println(address.State)
fmt.Println(address.Zip)
fmt.Println(address.Country)
fmt.Println(address.Latitude)
fmt.Println(address.Longitude)

fmt.Println(contact.Phone)
fmt.Println(contact.Email)

fmt.Println(creditCard.Type)
fmt.Println(creditCard.Number)
fmt.Println(creditCard.Exp)
fmt.Println(creditCard.Cvv)

// Output: Priscilla
// Thornton
// male
// 20
// 575624882
// Cosplaying
// Qado Energy, Inc.
// Nail Technician
// Technical
// Quality
// 79993 Lanebury, Lincoln, Kansas 93050
// 79993 Lanebury
// Lincoln
// Kansas
// 93050
// India
// -26.936948
// -28.374174
// 2689405915
// elsareynolds@nichols.io
// American Express
// 4570938757201747
// 11/29
// 205
```

```
male
20

Cosplaying

Technical
Quality

93050
India
-26.936948
-28.374174
2689405915
elsareynolds@nichols.io
American Express

11/29
```

<a name="Song"></a>
### func [Song](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/song.go#L27>)

```go
func Song() *SongInfo
```



<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
Seed(11)
song := Song()
fmt.Println(song.Name)
fmt.Println(song.Artist)
fmt.Println(song.Genre)

// Output: What Was I Made For?
// Taylor Swift
// Country
```

#### Output

```
What Was I Made For?
Taylor Swift
Country
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="TemplateOptions"></a>
## type [TemplateOptions](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/template.go#L15-L18>)

TemplateOptions defines values needed for template document generation

```go
type TemplateOptions struct {
    Funcs template.FuncMap `fake:"-"`
    Data  any              `json:"data" xml:"data" fake:"-"`
}
```

<a name="XMLOptions"></a>
## type [XMLOptions](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/xml.go#L12-L19>)

XMLOptions defines values needed for json generation

```go
type XMLOptions struct {
    Type          string  `json:"type" xml:"type" fake:"{randomstring:[array,single]}"` // single or array
    RootElement   string  `json:"root_element" xml:"root_element"`
    RecordElement string  `json:"record_element" xml:"record_element"`
    RowCount      int     `json:"row_count" xml:"row_count" fake:"{number:1,10}"`
    Indent        bool    `json:"indent" xml:"indent"`
    Fields        []Field `json:"fields" xml:"fields" fake:"{fields}"`
}
```

# data

```go
import "github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/v7/data"
```

## Index

- [Constants](<#constants>)
- [Variables](<#variables>)
- [func Get\(key string\) map\[string\]\[\]string](<#Get>)
- [func GetSubData\(key, subkey string\) \[\]string](<#GetSubData>)
- [func List\(\) map\[string\]\[\]string](<#List>)
- [func Remove\(key string\)](<#Remove>)
- [func RemoveSub\(key, subkey string\)](<#RemoveSub>)
- [func Set\(key string, data map\[string\]\[\]string\)](<#Set>)
- [func SetSub\(key, subkey string, data \[\]string\)](<#SetSub>)
- [type CreditCardCode](<#CreditCardCode>)
- [type CreditCardInfo](<#CreditCardInfo>)
- [type ISBNRule](<#ISBNRule>)

<a name="ISBN13Prefix"></a>Prefixes for ISBN standards

```go
const (
    ISBN13Prefix = "978"
    ISBN10Prefix = "979"
)
```

<a name="WordKeys"></a>

```go
var WordKeys []string
```

<a name="Get"></a>
## func [Get](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/data/data.go#L57>)

```go
func Get(key string) map[string][]string
```



<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
// Get data
data := Get("person")

// Print the first name
fmt.Println(data["prefix"])

// Output:
// [Mr. Mrs. Ms. Miss Dr.]
```

#### Output

```
[Mr. Mrs. Ms. Miss Dr.]
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Sub)</summary>
<p>



```go
// Get data
data := GetSubData("person", "prefix")

// Print the first name
fmt.Println(data[0])

// Output:
// Mr.
```

#### Output

```
Mr.
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Set"></a>
## func [Set](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/data/data.go#L66>)

```go
func Set(key string, data map[string][]string)
```



<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
// Add data
Set("desserts", map[string][]string{
	"cake":      {"chocolate", "vanilla"},
	"pie":       {"apple", "pecan"},
	"ice cream": {"strawberry", "vanilla"},
})
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="SetSub"></a>
## func [SetSub](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/data/data.go#L83>)

```go
func SetSub(key, subkey string, data []string)
```



<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
// Add data
SetSub("desserts", "cake", []string{"chocolate", "vanilla"})
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="CreditCardCode"></a>
## type [CreditCardCode](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/data/payment.go#L13-L16>)

CreditCardCode contains code type and size

```go
type CreditCardCode struct {
    Name string
    Size uint
}
```

<a name="CreditCardInfo"></a>
## type [CreditCardInfo](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/data/payment.go#L4-L10>)

CreditCardInfo contains credit card info

```go
type CreditCardInfo struct {
    Display  string
    Patterns []uint
    Gaps     []uint
    Lengths  []uint
    Code     CreditCardCode
}
```

<a name="ISBNRule"></a>
## type [ISBNRule](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/data/isbn.go#L10-L14>)

ISBNRule defines a registrant rule range and its length

```go
type ISBNRule struct {
    Min    string
    Max    string
    Length int
}
```

# source

```go
import "github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/v7/source"
```

## Index

- [type Crypto](<#Crypto>)
  - [func NewCrypto\(\) \*Crypto](<#NewCrypto>)
  - [func \(s \*Crypto\) Uint64\(\) uint64](<#Crypto.Uint64>)
- [type Dumb](<#Dumb>)
  - [func NewDumb\(seed uint64\) \*Dumb](<#NewDumb>)
  - [func \(d \*Dumb\) Seed\(seed uint64\)](<#Dumb.Seed>)
  - [func \(d \*Dumb\) Uint64\(\) uint64](<#Dumb.Uint64>)
- [type JSF](<#JSF>)
  - [func NewJSF\(seed uint64\) \*JSF](<#NewJSF>)
  - [func \(jsf \*JSF\) Seed\(seed uint64\)](<#JSF.Seed>)
  - [func \(jsf \*JSF\) Uint64\(\) uint64](<#JSF.Uint64>)
- [type SFC](<#SFC>)
  - [func NewSFC\(seed uint64\) \*SFC](<#NewSFC>)
  - [func \(s \*SFC\) Seed\(seed uint64\)](<#SFC.Seed>)
  - [func \(s \*SFC\) Uint64\(\) uint64](<#SFC.Uint64>)


<a name="Crypto"></a>
## type [Crypto](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/source/crypto.go#L23-L26>)



```go
type Crypto struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewCrypto"></a>
### func [NewCrypto](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/source/crypto.go#L29>)

```go
func NewCrypto() *Crypto
```

NewCrypto creates a new instance of Crypto.

<a name="Crypto.Uint64"></a>
### func \(\*Crypto\) [Uint64](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/source/crypto.go#L45>)

```go
func (s *Crypto) Uint64() uint64
```

Uint64 generates a pseudo\-random 64\-bit value using crypto/rand, served from a buffered block of data.

<a name="Dumb"></a>
## type [Dumb](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/source/dumb.go#L20-L22>)

Dumb is a simplistic generator for predictable testing.

```go
type Dumb struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewDumb"></a>
### func [NewDumb](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/source/dumb.go#L26>)

```go
func NewDumb(seed uint64) *Dumb
```

NewDumb initializes a Dumb generator. If the seed is 0, initializes with the current timestamp.

<a name="Dumb.Seed"></a>
### func \(\*Dumb\) [Seed](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/source/dumb.go#L33>)

```go
func (d *Dumb) Seed(seed uint64)
```

Seed sets the generator's state. If the seed is 0, it uses the current timestamp as the seed.

<a name="Dumb.Uint64"></a>
### func \(\*Dumb\) [Uint64](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/source/dumb.go#L41>)

```go
func (d *Dumb) Uint64() uint64
```

Uint64 returns the next number in the sequence, incrementing the state.

```go
type JSF struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewJSF"></a>
### func [NewJSF](<https://github.com/brianvoe/gofakeit/blob/master/source/jsf.go#L22>)

```go
func NewJSF(seed uint64) *JSF
```

NewJSF creates and returns a new JSF pseudo\-random number generator.
